text:
Your working directory is a checkout of the real react-datepicker repository at the commit where the bug below reproduces. Find and fix the bug in the library source.

Bug: react-datepicker's date parser accepts a `strictParsing` prop. Previously, when `strictParsing` is false and a typed/pasted date string doesn't exactly match the configured `dateFormat`, the parser fell back to the JS engine's native `new Date(string)` constructor, which understands many common date/time formats regardless of `dateFormat`. That fallback is currently missing: with `strictParsing` false, `parseDate` (exported from `src/date_utils.ts`) now returns `null` for such flexible input instead of a valid `Date`. For example `parseDate("2025-12-16", "MM/dd/yyyy", undefined, false)` should resolve to December 16, 2025 (native `Date` can parse the ISO string even though it doesn't match `dateFormat`), but currently returns null. Two things must NOT regress while fixing this: when `strictParsing` is true there should be no native-parsing fallback (non-matching input still returns null), and when the input does match `dateFormat` exactly, that strict format-based result should still be preferred over the native fallback.

Environment notes: no root access is needed. Dependencies are already installed via `corepack enable && yarn install --immutable`. Run tests with Jest, e.g. `node_modules/.bin/jest src/test/date_utils_test.test.ts` to scope to the date-parsing tests. Fix the library source; do not just add workarounds in test files.

`--dangerously-bypass-hook-trust` is enabled. Enabled hooks may run without review for this invocation.

text: I’ll inspect the current parser and its focused tests, then patch the library implementation and run the date-utils Jest suite. I’ll also check prior Nowledge context for this parser behavior before concluding, as requested by the workspace instructions.

input.query: react-datepicker parseDate strictParsing native Date fallback dateFormat

The shared CalendarContainer now renders translate=\"no\" on the calendar dialog element, preventing Safari and similar browser translation passes from rewriting live React-managed calendar text nodes. The container regression test asserts the attribute; focused Jest and TypeScript checks pass.","relevance_score":0.8514999999999999,"relevance_reason":"Semantic Match (46%) + Text Match (28%) + Keyword Match (27%)"},{"id":"a143c07e-456e-41c6-8438-c4cfb2028177","memory_id":"a143c07e-456e-41c6-8438-c4cfb2028177","title":"react-datepicker monthHeaderPosition implementation","content":"Implemented monthHeaderPosition as a public DatePicker/Calendar prop with top (default), middle, and bottom modes. Top preserves the existing month-container header and calendar-wide navigation; middle/bottom pass each month’s header into Month around/below its day grid, with localized previous/next buttons and month-date-based min/max disabled checks. TypeScript and focused Calendar/DatePicker Jest suites pass.","relevance_score":0.8474999999999999,"relevance_reason":"Semantic Match (42%) + Text Match (33%) + Keyword Match (26%)"},{"id":"e4fae2ba-9738-42af-80e7-e9a191604a67","memory_id":"e4fae2ba-9738-42af-80e7-e9a191604a67","title":"react-datepicker multi-month custom header navigation reset","content":"In react-datepicker, day selection stores the visible panel index in DatePicker state.monthSelectedIn, which Calendar uses to offset the rendered months. A custom header's changeMonth callback changes Calendar's internal date but does not clear that index. The fix is to adapt renderCustomHeader in DatePicker and reset monthSelectedIn to 0 before delegating to the Calendar callback, preserving panel-origin behavior for day selection while making custom month jumps start at the leftmost panel. Regression coverage belongs in the DatePicker test suite with monthsShown={2}, a day click in the second month, and a custom-header changeMonth assertion.","relevance_score":0.8324176076107469,"relevance_reason":"Semantic Match (46%) + Keyword Match (28%) + Text Match (27%)"},{"id":"70599eb5-3c17-466e-84fe-1e457217a170","memory_id":"70599eb5-3c17-466e-84fe-1e457217a170","title":"Exclude adjacent-month filler cells from range preview","content":"In react-datepicker, Day.isInSelectingRange must first verify that the cell date's month matches the Month component's month prop. Calendar grids include adjacent-month filler cells, and date-range interval checks alone otherwise mark those cells with react-datepicker__day--in-selecting-range. The guard belongs in src/day.tsx so it also prevents selecting-range start/end modifiers from being applied to filler cells. Standalone Day tests must provide a realistic month prop (getMonth(day)).","relevance_score":0.7400608148183505,"relevance_reason":"Semantic Match (51%) + Text Match (27%) + Keyword Match (22%)"},{"id":"88046863-56eb-4f1f-8d0e-7de6a05ce5af","memory_id":"88046863-56eb-4f1f-8d0e-7de6a05ce5af","title":"Downshift aria-selected props are booleans","content":"Downshift useCombobox and useSelect getItemProps now return boolean aria-selected values directly from their comparisons. The prior template-literal coercion produced truthy string values; DOM aria-selected serialization remains unchanged. Focused regression assertions and the full Jest suite pass.","relevance_score":0.5105595635473728,"relevance_reason":"Semantic Match (100%)"}],"total":5,"query":"react-datepicker parseDate strictParsing native Date fallback dateFormat","retrieval_mode":"strict","retrieval_space_ids":["default"]}

input.command: /bin/bash -lc "sed -n '1,240p' src/date_utils.ts && rg -n \"parseDate|strictParsing|native|new Date\" src/test/date_utils_test.test.ts src | head -120"
/bin/bash -lc "rg -n -C 25 \"export function parseDate|function parseDate\" src/date_utils.ts && sed -n '990,1130p' src/test/date_utils_test.test.ts && git status --short"

text: The bug is localized to `parseDate`: it currently exhausts the configured `date-fns` formats and immediately returns `null`. The intended ordering is straightforward—return the first valid format-based parse, then only in non-strict mode try `new Date(value)`, accepting it only if valid. I’m checking the focused suite behavior before applying that small source-only fix.

text: The focused suite exposed one existing expectation that conflicts with the requested restored behavior: the test expects `"26. feb 1995"` to remain `null` under a Portuguese locale, but the native constructor validly parses it. That is exactly the fallback behavior described in the bug, so I’m checking repository history and project conventions to recover the intended upstream implementation rather than weakening the fix to satisfy the stale assertion.

text: Repository history confirms the original implementation used this exact fallback only when non-strict format parsing failed, while strict parsing never invoked `new Date(value)`. I’ve restored that ordering in `src/date_utils.ts`. The one failing legacy assertion is incompatible with the requested semantics because Node’s native parser accepts that string; I’ll now run type/lint checks and verify the explicit ISO example plus strict behavior directly.

input.title: react-datepicker parseDate restores non-strict native fallback
input.content: In react-datepicker src/date_utils.ts, parseDate should return the first valid configured date-fns format parse, then only when strictParsing is false fall back to new Date(value) if that native result is valid. Strict parsing must return null for non-matching input. Repository history confirms this was the prior behavior; one legacy locale test expecting null for a natively parseable string conflicts with the restored semantics.

text:
Fixed `parseDate` in [src/date_utils.ts](/home/user/workspace/src/date_utils.ts:321).

- Non-strict parsing now falls back to valid `new Date(value)` results.
- Strict parsing never uses the native fallback.
- Exact format matches remain preferred.

Validation: ESLint, TypeScript checks, and targeted parsing tests pass. The full date-utils suite has one legacy assertion conflicting with the restored native parsing behavior.
